q.en: What assignment does Mr. Bolstead give his class?
q.zh: 博尔斯特德先生给全班布置了什么任务？

options.en: To start their own business in a classroom mini-economy.
options.zh: 在课堂的迷你经济体系中创办自己的小生意。

options.en: To memorize the multiplication tables.
To write a book report.
To plant a school garden.

explain.en: Mr. Bolstead announces the unit assignment is to 'start your own business' within a mini-economy that teaches 'how capitalism works.'
explain.zh: 博尔斯特德先生宣布本单元任务是'创办你自己的生意'，在一个教授'资本主义如何运作'的迷你经济体系里进行。

q.en: What are 'Bolstead bucks' and how do students earn them?
q.zh: 什么是'博尔斯特德币'？学生们如何赚取？

options.en: Real dollars given by parents.
School money earned at one Bolstead buck per star for helping teachers or janitors, used to buy at the booths.
options.zh: 帮助老师或校工每得一颗星就赚一博尔斯特德币的校内钱币，用于在摊位上购买。

options.en: Coins handed out by a vending machine.
options.zh: 自动售货机吐出来的硬币。

options.en: Points given for good test scores.

explain.en: Mr. Bolstead says students 'get one Bolstead buck for every star' earned helping out teachers or janitors, and they use them to buy goods next Friday.
explain.zh: 博尔斯特德先生说，学生'每帮老师或校工一次得一星，就赚一博尔斯特德币'，并在下周五用来买东西。

q.en: How much start-up capital does each group receive?
q.zh: 每个小组获得了多少启动资金？

options.en: 20 Bolstead bucks.
500 Bolstead bucks.
10 Bolstead bucks.
Five twenty-dollar bills, equal to 100 Bolstead bucks.
options.zh: 五张二十元的纸币，合计100博尔斯特德币。

explain.en: Mr. Bolstead hands each group 'five twenty-dollar Bolstead bucks' and calls it 'one hundred Bolstead bucks' of capital.
explain.zh: 博尔斯特德先生发给每组'五张二十元的博尔斯特德币'，并称其为100博尔斯特德币的'资本'。

q.en: What product does Leon's group finally decide to sell?
q.zh: 利昂的小组最终决定卖什么产品？

options.en: Paper dolls.
Skateboards.
Snowboards.
Peppermint patties (candy mints).

explain.en: After noticing the school removed candy machines, they choose peppermint patties because 'students really want—something they can't get at school.'
explain.zh: 发现学校撤掉了糖果机后，他们选了薄荷糖，因为那是'学生们真正想要、在学校买不到的东西'。

q.en: Why did the group choose candy as their product?
q.zh: 小组为什么选择糖果作为产品？

options.en: Shelly's homework told them to.
options.zh: 谢利的作业这么要求的。

options.en: Marcus wanted to make snowboards.
The school had removed candy machines, and students wanted candy they couldn't get at school (supply and demand).
options.zh: 学校撤掉了糖果机，学生们想要在学校买不到的糖果（供需关系）。

options.en: They all loved doing math.

explain.en: Leon points out the school removed candy vending machines, so candy is something students 'can't get at school'—a real demand he links to supply and demand.
explain.zh: 利昂指出学校撤掉了糖果自动售货机，所以糖果是学生们'在学校买不到'的东西——他把这和需求与供给联系起来。

q.en: Using pizza as an example, what does the book say about supply and demand?
q.zh: 以披萨为例，书中关于供需关系说了什么？

options.en: When many pizza places exist, supply matches or exceeds demand, so pizza costs less.
options.zh: 当披萨店很多时，供给与需求持平或超出，所以披萨更便宜。

options.en: When supply is high, prices always go up.
options.zh: 供给高时，价格总是上涨。

options.en: Demand has nothing to do with price.
Pizza is free in a capitalist country.
options.zh: 在资本主义国家披萨是免费的。

explain.en: Mr. Bolstead explains that with many pizza places, 'the supply of pizza has probably matched or exceeded the demand,' so 'pizza costs less.'
explain.zh: 博尔斯特德先生解释说，披萨店很多时，'披萨的供给与需求持平或超过'，因此'披萨更便宜'。

options.en: The money borrowed to start a business.
A person who buys or rents goods.
The money earned from a business after all costs are paid.
options.zh: 扣除所有成本后，从生意中赚到的钱。

options.en: Money paid by a borrower for using loaned money.
options.zh: 借款人因使用贷款而支付的钱。

explain.en: The glossary defines 'profit' as 'the money earned from a business or investment after all costs are paid.'
explain.zh: 词汇表把 profit 定义为'扣除所有成本后，从生意或投资中赚到的钱'。

q.en: What is the author's main purpose in telling this story?
q.zh: 作者讲这个故事的主要目的是什么？

options.en: To give a real peppermint patty recipe only.
options.zh: 仅仅为了给出一个真正的薄荷糖配方。

options.en: To teach basic economics—capitalism, supply and demand, and profit—through a classroom story.
options.zh: 通过一个课堂故事，教授资本主义、供需和利润等基础经济知识。

options.en: To persuade readers to become millionaires.
options.zh: 劝说读者成为百万富翁。

options.en: To describe a pizza party.

explain.en: The story wraps real economic lessons (capital, interest, supply/demand, profit, break-even) inside a fictional classroom project.
explain.zh: 故事把真实的经济知识（资本、利息、供需、利润、盈亏平衡）包裹在一个虚构的课堂项目里。

q.en: What name does the group give its business, and who wins the competition?
q.zh: 小组给自己的生意起了什么名字？谁赢得了比赛？

options.en: Paper Dolls Inc.—they win.
Snowboard Shop—they win.
They never named it and lost.
options.zh: 他们从没起名，而且输了。

options.en: Little Dollies' Mints—they win.
options.zh: 小娃娃薄荷糖——他们赢了。

explain.en: Marcus's sign reads 'Little Dollies' Mints,' and Mr. Bolstead announces 'Little Dollies' Mints!' as the winner.
explain.zh: 马库斯做的招牌写着'小娃娃薄荷糖'，博尔斯特德先生宣布获胜者是'小娃娃薄荷糖！'

q.en: According to the glossary, what is 'capital'?
q.zh: 根据词汇表，'capital'（资本）是什么？

options.en: Money or property used to start or run a business.
options.zh: 用于开办或经营企业的资金或财产。

options.en: The need or desire for a good among people who can pay.
options.zh: 有能力付费的人对某商品的需求或欲望。

options.en: An economic system controlled by the government.
options.zh: 由政府控制的经济体系。

options.en: A person who buys goods or services.

explain.en: The glossary defines 'capital' as 'money or property used to start or run a business.'
explain.zh: 词汇表把 capital 定义为'用于开办或经营企业的资金或财产'。

prompt.en: Leon's group succeeded by spotting a real demand (candy the school no longer sold) and adding a clever extra—selling the recipe. In your own words, explain how the ideas of supply, demand, and profit worked together to make 'Little Dollies' Mints' the winning business, and why selling the recipes was 'a stroke of genius.'
prompt.zh: 利昂的小组靠发现真实需求（学校不再卖的糖果）并加了一个聪明的点子——卖配方——而获胜。用自己的话说说，供给、需求和利润这三者如何共同让'小娃娃薄荷糖'成为赢家，并解释为什么卖配方是'神来之笔'。

en: Use the supply-and-demand discussion: the school removed candy machines, so demand was high and supply at school was zero.
Remember Mr. Bolstead's definition of profit (money made minus all costs) and that the extra recipe sales boosted total profit.
zh: 运用供需讨论：学校撤掉了糖果机，所以需求高、校内供给为零。
记住博尔斯特德对利润的定义（赚的钱减去所有成本），而额外卖配方提高了总利润。
